description_long: This pole is a cylindrical wooden structure with a carved face and circular motif on its front, resembling a totem or decorative post. The wood surface is textured with visible grain, cracks, and some greenish moss or lichen patches, giving it an aged, weathered appearance. The front features two round, blue-rimmed eyes and a simple mouth, with a larger circular carving below, possibly representing a stylized belly or emblem. The sides reveal the depth of the carvings, with protruding elements for the nose and mouth, while the back is unadorned and shows only the natural wood texture. Faint white markings or scratches are scattered across the surface, adding to its rustic character. This object would likely be found in parks, themed urban spaces, or as a decorative element in communal areas.
description: A carved wooden pole with a face and circular motif, featuring weathered textures, mossy patches, and faint white markings. The front is decorated, while the back is plain wood.
description_view_0: The front view shows the carved face with blue-rimmed eyes, a mouth, and a circular motif below, along with visible wood grain and mossy patches.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the protruding nose and mouth carvings, as well as the depth of the engravings and some white markings.
description_view_2: The rear view displays an unadorned, cylindrical wooden surface with natural grain, cracks, and faint moss or lichen.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the profile of the face carvings and the continuation of the wood's texture and markings.
